message:
Last week, our power went out for maybe six hours—nothing dramatic, just a downed line two blocks over. But the *group chat* that followed was absolutely hilarious. My sister asking "do we have flashlights?" My brother responding with a photo of three dead batteries. My mom going "I TOLD YOU we needed a plan." And me frantically Googling where I put our important documents.

It got me thinking: we talk about emergency prep like it's this big, serious thing. But honestly? It's just about having a few boring basics sorted so you're not scrambling when something actually happens. Nothing fancy. Just knowing where your water is, keeping some cash nearby, maybe a folder with the stuff you'd actually need.

Turns out my family group chat is *way* more fun once we've got our ducks in a row.

What's something in your home prep that you've been meaning to get around to? This little refresher actually helped me finally do mine.
